references.title: Evaluation of early student performance prediction given concept drift

extracted.Courses: 3 editions of an Introductory Physiology course
extracted.Data sources:
LMS logs,
MSLQ,
Demographics,
Acdemic Background
extracted.Features: [Engagement Features, Performance Features, Participation Features, Regularity Features, Repetition Features, Learning Profile Features]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors aggregate the different types of features at their disposal into one of 6 categories.

All data is collectec up to week 8, which represents the course’s halfway point. 

Moreover, the authors attempt different feature sets.
extracted.Models:
TrialExamPure (OneR),
Single-Feature LogisticREgression,
2-Feature Logistic Regression,
LASSO,
Logistic Regression with features selected from LASSO,
XGBoost, Random Forest, SVM, 
XGBoostLasso, RFLasso, SVMLasso
extracted.Performance Metric: Best Model:
{Accuracy: 0.55 - 2Feature Logistic, Precision_High: 0.94 - 1Feature Logistic, Precision_Medium: 0.39 - 2Feature Logistic, Precision_Low: 0.72 - 2Feature Logistic, Recall_High: 0.59 - OneR, Recall_Medium: 1.00 - SVM & SVMLASSO, Recall_
Low: 0.99 - RF}
extracted.Comments:
Authors make a comparison of different feature sets. In addition, two distinct approaches are attempted - Classification and Regression.

Results show that there is value in using MSLQ as it does seem that high values on elaboration are predictors of high marks.

Surprisingly, a simple Lasso Regression is capable of outperforming algorithms that are argued to be more powerful.
extracted.Limitations:
Small sample size, witht he test data belonging to a context that substantially different than the one used for the training data (Covid vs post-Covid).

Teacher behavior and influence can have an effect on the behavior and grades of their students.

No capture of offline learning activities.

In addition, two distinct approaches are attempted - Classification and Regression.Results show that there is value in using MSLQ as it does seem that high values on elaboration are predictors of high marks.Surprisingly, a simple Lasso Regression is capable of outperforming algorithms that are argued to be more powerful when in the rpesence of concept drift.

annotations.text: ML pipelines are essential to predict students in need and make necessary recommendations often using behavioral logs or demographic data stored in the SIS. This strategy is vulnerable to concept drift or spuriousness.

Authors show that Lasso outperforms multiple models in both scenarios. Moreover, a linear regression with 2 new features (handcrafted) outperforms multiple more complex ML models. Authors propose their results as an interesting benchmark for future studies.
